Oracle Events
OE-1: Search Slack for Samira's readiness sweep message using conversations_search_messages (query related to "ExpenseBot relaunch" or "readiness sweep" or "April 30"). Discovers Samira's post in #engineering (C003) assigning Lena the readiness sweep with 7 verification areas and the April 30 relaunch target.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. Query not given in prompt -> candidate for QC.
OE-2: Read the thread on Samira's Slack message using conversations_replies (channel_id=C003, thread_ts of Samira's message). Discovers Dmitri's reply about the multi-currency edge case: PolicyValidator treats "CAD 450.00" as 450 USD and rejects it against the $400 limit instead of converting to ~USD 326.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. thread_ts derived from OE-1 -> skip QC.
OE-3: Search emails for Samira's readiness sweep checklist using search_emails (query related to "readiness sweep" or "ExpenseBot relaunch" or "checklist"). Finds Samira's email (email_samira_readiness_sweep) with the full verification checklist covering config, pipeline, testing, launch readiness, data cleanup (specifically Andre Washington's $1,200 desk), dashboard, and invoice safety.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. Query not given in prompt -> candidate for QC.
OE-4: Search Slack for Anh's message about the dashboard validation regression using conversations_search_messages (query related to "Anh" or "dashboard" or "validation" or "decimal" or "ENG-218"). Discovers Anh's post in #engineering (C003) reporting that the policy editor rejects decimal max_amount values (e.g., 599.99) due to a regex bug (^\d+$ instead of ^\d+(\.\d+)?$).
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. Query not given in prompt -> candidate for QC.
OE-5: Search emails for Elena's message about the invoice safety ticket using search_emails (query related to "invoice safety" or "deprioritized" or "ENG-187"). Finds Elena's email (email_elena_invoice_safety_followup) to Samira CC Lena, asking why ENG-187 was deprioritized, referencing the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. Query not given in prompt -> candidate for QC.
OE-6: Search Linear for ExpenseBot project issues using linear_list_issues (project="proj_expense_autocategorizer" or query related to "ExpenseBot"). Returns tickets: ENG-214 (Vectral config, issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (Mosaic config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, issue_pipeline_hardening), ENG-218 (dashboard validation, issue_dashboard_type_validation), ENG-219 (launch readiness, issue_launch_readiness_checklist), ENG-216 (audit, issue_audit_expenses), ENG-212 (testing), ENG-220 (duplicate receipts).
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool -> candidate for TS.
OE-7: Read comments on ENG-217 using linear_list_comments (issueId="issue_pipeline_hardening"). Discovers Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_multicurrency_blocker) with technical details: validateAmount() doesn't extract currency code, test also used wrong limit ($400 old default vs $600 corrected), proposes two resolution approaches.
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool -> candidate for TS.
OE-8: Read comments on ENG-187 using linear_list_comments (issueId="linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2"). Discovers Lena's April 6 comment deferring ENG-187 due to DOT compliance work (Raj Patel interstate move), Samira's acknowledgment, Marcus's escalations about the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice mismatch, and Elena's manager directive requesting immediate ownership and a shippable stopgap.
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool (same as OE-7, one TS covers both) -> skip additional TS.
OE-9: Get details of individual tickets using linear_get_issue for ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening), ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config), ENG-215 (issue_p1_mosaic_malformed), ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation), ENG-219 (issue_launch_readiness_checklist), ENG-187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2). Checks current state, assignee, and description for each to determine actual status vs. ticket status.
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool (same as OE-6 family) -> skip additional TS.
OE-10: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases, then list tables using airtable_list_tables (base_id="appMoveOpsOps001"). Discovers tblStipends00001 ("Stipend Transactions") table.
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool -> candidate for TS.
OE-11: Search Airtable for pilot stipend records using airtable_search_records (base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Stipend Transactions", field_name="Employee Name", value="Andre Washington") or airtable_list_records to browse all records. Finds record recStip000000021: Andre Washington, Standing Desk, $1,200, Approval Status="Approved", with no error notes indicating this was a false approval from the pilot.
-> Read/lookup action. Non-default tool (same as OE-10 family) -> skip additional TS. Query not given in prompt (agent must infer "Andre Washington" from Samira's email checklist) -> candidate for QC.
OE-11b: Search Airtable for additional false-approval records beyond Andre Washington. Discover recStip000000023 (Kenji Nakamura, Coworking Day Passes, $135, Approval Status "Approved") which has the same excluded_categories config bug. Dmitri's comment on ENG-216 (comment_dmitri_airtable_cleanup_reminder) explicitly flags this as a second record needing correction. Agent must find this either by browsing Airtable records or by reading Dmitri's comment
OE-12: Look up contact details for Samira using contacts_search_contacts (query="Samira"). Returns samira.tariq@moveops.com.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. Query obvious -> skip QC.
OE-13: Look up Slack channel for #engineering using channels_list. Identifies C003 as the engineering channel.
-> Read/lookup action. Default tool -> skip TS. No query parameter -> skip QC.
OE-14: Add comment to ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) about the multi-currency blocker using linear_create_comment (issueId="issue_pipeline_hardening", body summarizing multi-currency edge case findings, Dmitri's test failure, and proposed resolution).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE-15: Add comment to ENG-218 (dashboard validation) about the decimal validation regression using linear_create_comment (issueId="issue_dashboard_type_validation", body summarizing Anh's regex regression with decimal max_amount values and need to align frontend/backend validation).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE-16: Add comment to ENG-187 (invoice safety) with deprioritization context using linear_create_comment (issueId="linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", body explaining why it was deprioritized: March config crisis, April 6 DOT compliance deferral, current status, and proposed timeline).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE-17: Update stale Linear ticket statuses using linear_update_issue for tickets where actual work status differs from ticket status (e.g., ENG-214, ENG-215 if config fixes are complete but tickets still show in_progress).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1.
OE-18: Update Andre Washington's $1,200 standing desk Airtable record using airtable_update_records (base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_id="tblStipends00001", record id="recStip000000021", fields: Approval Status changed from "Approved" to "Under Review" or similar corrected status, with review notes explaining it was a false approval from the pilot).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE-18b: Update Kenji Nakamura's coworking Airtable record using airtable_update_records (record id recStip000000023, Approval Status changed from "Approved" to "Under Review" or similar, with review notes).
OE-19: Send readiness report email to Samira using send_email (sender="lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com", recipients=["samira.tariq@moveops.com"], content covering each checklist area: config verification, pipeline hardening with multi-currency blocker, testing, launch readiness checklist, data cleanup actions, dashboard validation regression, and invoice safety ticket context for Elena).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.
OE-20: Post readiness status summary in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id="C003", payload summarizing the relaunch readiness findings and current blockers for team visibility).
-> Write action -> Outcome 1.1 + 1.2.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A comment was created (via linear_create_comment) on ENG-217 (issueId containing "issue_pipeline_hardening" or issue number 217) and the comment body mentions the multi-currency issue, specifically that currency-prefixed amounts like "CAD 450.00" are not handled correctly by the PolicyValidator (or similar description of the edge case).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening', and the body explicitly describes the multi-currency edge case: 'test_multi_currency_cad_ergonomic FAILS — the validateAmount() function does a naive string-to-number conversion that strips the currency prefix... "CAD 450.00" is compared as 450 against a USD $400 limit, causing a false rejection.'
✓Run #2Item 183 shows linear_create_comment on issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening' with body explicitly mentioning the multi-currency edge case: 'CAD 450.00' is compared as 450 against a USD limit of $400, causing a false rejection. Criterion is met.
✗Run #3The agent used `linear_update_issue` on `issue_pipeline_hardening` to document the multi-currency finding (updating the issue description), but the criterion requires `linear_create_comment`. No `linear_create_comment` tool call was made on this ticket at any point in the trajectory.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening' and the body explicitly states '**Multi-currency edge case** (flagged by Dmitri on April 25): The `validateAmount()` type guard strips non-numeric prefixes but doesn't handle currency codes. A CAD 450.00 expense gets compared as 450 against the USD limit, causing a false rejection.'
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening' (ENG-217) and the body explicitly mentions the multi-currency edge case, 'CAD 450.00', currency prefix stripping, and PolicyValidator's naive conversion causing false rejection.
✓Run #6A linear_create_comment call was made with issueId 'issue_pipeline_hardening' and the body explicitly mentions the multi-currency edge case: 'Multi-currency amounts: validateAmount() strips non-numeric prefixes (e.g., "CAD ") but discards the currency code entirely. This means a CAD 450 expense gets compared against a USD 400 limit as if it were 450 USD, resulting in a false rejection.'
2 A comment was created (via linear_create_comment) on ENG-218 (issueId containing "issue_dashboard_type_validation" or issue number 218) and the comment body mentions the decimal validation regression, specifically that the policy editor rejects decimal max_amount values like 599.99 (or similar description of the regex bug).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a linear_create_comment call with issueId 'issue_dashboard_type_validation', and the body explicitly describes the decimal regression: 'Decimal amounts are rejected — the numeric validation regex uses ^\d+$ instead of ^\d+(\.\d+)?$. So 599.99 fails validation even though it's a valid amount.'
✓Run #2Item 185 shows linear_create_comment on issueId 'issue_dashboard_type_validation' with body explicitly mentioning the decimal validation regression: max_amount regex uses ^\d+$ which rejects valid decimal values like 599.99. Criterion is met.
✗Run #3The agent used `linear_update_issue` on `issue_dashboard_type_validation` to document the decimal regex bug, but the criterion requires `linear_create_comment`. No `linear_create_comment` tool call was made on this ticket. The content about the decimal validation issue (599.99 rejected by `^\d+$`) was added to the issue description, not as a comment.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_dashboard_type_validation'. The body mentions '**Decimal validation regression**: The numeric validator regex uses `^\d+$` instead of `^\d+(\.\d+)?$`, causing it to reject valid decimal amounts like `599.99`.' This matches the criterion.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_comment with issueId 'issue_dashboard_type_validation' (ENG-218) and the body explicitly mentions the decimal regression, that the regex uses '^\d+$' instead of '^\d+(\.\d+)?$', and cites 599.99 as an example of a rejected decimal value.
✓Run #6A linear_create_comment call was made with issueId 'issue_dashboard_type_validation' and the body explicitly mentions the decimal validation regression: 'Anh deployed to staging and found that the max_amount field validation uses ^\d+$ regex, which rejects valid decimal amounts like 599.99.'
3 A comment was created (via linear_create_comment) on ENG-187 (issueId containing "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2" or issue number 187) and the comment body explains the deprioritization reason, referencing the March config crisis or the DOT compliance work (or both) as the reason the ticket was deferred.
outcome

11 The final response identifies the multi-currency edge case as a blocker or open issue for ENG-217, specifically that currency-prefixed amounts (e.g., CAD) are not handled correctly by the PolicyValidator.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly states: 'Dmitri wrote a multi-currency test for ENG-212 that exposes a real bug in your validateAmount() function. When an expense comes in as "CAD 450.00", the PolicyValidator strips the currency prefix and compares 450 against the USD $400 ergonomic limit → false rejection... This is a launch blocker.'
✓Run #2The final response explicitly identifies the multi-currency edge case as a relaunch blocker for ENG-217, specifically that 'CAD 450.00' → 450 causing false rejection against USD $400 limit, with PolicyValidator not handling currency prefixes.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly identifies the multi-currency edge case as a launch blocker: 'When an expense comes in as "CAD 450.00", your PolicyValidator.validateAmount() strips the currency prefix and compares 450 > 400 (the USD limit), rejecting it — even though CAD 450 ≈ USD 326, well within the limit. This is a launch blocker that needs currency-aware parsing in the type enforcement layer.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly identifies the multi-currency edge case under 'Dmitri's Edge Case (ENG-217)', stating that 'CAD 450.00 gets treated as 450 and compared against a USD limit — causing false rejections' and noting it as a blocker preventing the April 30 ship.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies the multi-currency edge case as a launch blocker for ENG-217, citing 'CAD 450.00', the PolicyValidator's naive string-to-number conversion, and explaining the false rejection mechanism.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies the multi-currency edge case as a blocker: 'Dmitri wrote a test showing that the PolicyValidator.validateAmount() function does a naive string-to-number conversion on currency-prefixed amounts (e.g., "CAD 450.00")... causing a false rejection. This directly affects your type enforcement work on ENG-217.'
12 The final response identifies the dashboard validation regression reported by Anh, specifically that the policy editor rejects decimal max_amount values (e.g., 599.99) on ENG-218.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly describes Anh's dashboard issue: 'Anh deployed dashboard type validation to staging but hit a regression: the numeric validation regex (^\d+$) rejects decimal amounts like $599.99. Quick fix — needs ^\d+(\.\d+)?$.'
✓Run #2The final response explicitly identifies Anh's dashboard validation regression: decimal regex `^\d+$` rejects values like 599.99, needs to be `^\d+(\.\d+)?$`. ENG-218 is identified as a blocker.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly describes Anh's frontend issue: 'Anh deployed dashboard type validation to staging but found the max_amount regex uses ^\d+$, which rejects valid decimals like 599.99.' It is labeled as a launch blocker for ENG-218.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly identifies Anh's issue under 'Anh's Frontend Issue (ENG-218)', stating that 'the numeric validator regex (`^\d+$`) rejects valid decimal amounts like `599.99`'.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies Anh's dashboard issue (ENG-218), noting the policy editor rejects decimal amounts like '599.99' because the regex uses '^\d+$' instead of '^\d+(\.\d+)?$', and labels it a launch blocker.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies Anh's dashboard regression: 'Anh's dashboard type validation uses ^\d+$ regex for max_amount, which rejects valid decimal values like 599.99... it's blocking.'
